page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6e-c12g1.3xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz with an x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 92.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 7.67 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is delivered via a single NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU with 32 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a clear contrast between CPU-only workloads and GPU-accelerated tasks. Single-core CPU performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% categories, respectively. Multi-core performance is more balanced, achieving average tier results across stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. However, the system excels in GPU-driven workloads. Large language model inference benchmarks demonstrate top-tier performance, with prompt processing and text generation for a 7B model reaching the top 10% of tested systems.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.gn6e-c12g1.3xlarge offers a specialized resource profile where the primary value lies in its high-capacity 32 GB VRAM GPU rather than raw CPU compute power. Because single-threaded CPU performance is a bottleneck, this instance is less suitable for general-purpose computing or CPU-bound serialization tasks. Instead, it is optimized for workloads that offload processing to the GPU, such as deep learning training, LLM inference for small to medium models, and GPU-accelerated data science pipelines.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6e-c12g1.3xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for workloads requiring dedicated hardware acceleration. It features 12 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 92.0 GB of system memory. The instance includes a single NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU with 32 GB of VRAM, while local storage is absent. Network performance is established at a 5 Gbps baseline. Benchmarks indicate that while single-core CPU performance is weak, multi-core CPU metrics are average, and GPU-driven tasks like LLM inference achieve top-tier results. This hardware profile makes the instance highly efficient for GPU-centric workloads, such as machine learning inference and data science tasks, where processing can be offloaded from the CPU to the high-capacity V100 GPU.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn6e-c12g1.3xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 92 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU with 32 GB VRAM for high-performance AI inference.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance combining dedicated Intel Xeon processors with an NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU for high-throughput machine learning inference.

Single NVIDIA Volta V100 GPU with 32 GB of dedicated VRAM
92.0 GB of system memory with 5 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier performance in LLM inference for small and medium models
No local storage bundled, relying on external storage solutions
